compiled_state.title: As a platform engineer joining a DSO team, I can read a comprehensive gap audit report identifying every missing, inaccurate, or incomplete item in root README, INSTALL, and CONFIGURATION-REFERENCE.md
compiled_state.author: Claude
compiled_state.description:
## Description

**What**: A comprehensive gap audit of three documentation artifacts (root README, current INSTALL at plugins/dso/docs/INSTALL.md, and CONFIGURATION-REFERENCE.md) that produces a written gap report with a command verification appendix and config key gap list, before any edits are made to those artifacts.
**Why**: The epic's audit-first approach requires knowing what is missing before writing. Without an audit, subsequent stories risk writing to the wrong target or duplicating existing content.
**Scope**:
- IN: Audit root README.md (existing content vs. SC requirements); audit plugins/dso/docs/INSTALL.md (existing commands, links, structure); document what root INSTALL.md should contain; audit CONFIGURATION-REFERENCE.md against all config keys used in the codebase; produce command verification appendix; produce config key gap list
- OUT: Editing any documentation file (Stories B, C, D, E); creating root INSTALL.md (Story C); updating CONFIGURATION-REFERENCE.md (Story E)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, a gap audit report exists documenting every item found missing, inaccurate, or incomplete across root README.md, plugins/dso/docs/INSTALL.md, and CONFIGURATION-REFERENCE.md
  <- Satisfies: "A gap audit report documents what is missing, inaccurate, or incomplete across README, INSTALL, and CONFIGURATION-REFERENCE.md before any edits are made"
- When this story is complete, the gap audit report includes a command verification appendix with: (a) results of executing each standalone shell command in the current INSTALL.md verbatim in a clean shell session; (b) a manual confirmation that the /plugin marketplace add and /plugin install commands match the syntax in the official plugin marketplace documentation
  <- Satisfies: "The report includes a command verification appendix"
- When this story is complete, the gap audit report includes a config key gap list produced by an automated scan of all files under plugins/dso/ AND .claude/dso-config.conf and the example config file for configuration key reads (including inline _read_config_key() function calls and raw grep/cut patterns against dso-config.conf)
  <- Satisfies: "An automated scan produces a gap list"

## Considerations

- [Maintainability] Config key scan must cover 4 locations: plugins/dso/ tree, .claude/dso-config.conf, example config file, inline _read_config_key() function calls (not only read-config.sh invocations)
- [Maintainability] Config key scan must also detect raw grep/cut patterns against dso-config.conf that read specific key names without using read-config.sh or _read_config_key() (the config system is grep/cut-based; scripts may inline their own key reads)

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue -- one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.

comments.body: Story complete: all 3 children closed, completion-verifier PASS on all 5 done definitions.
comments.author: Test
